edges.target: Somatic FGFR1 gain-of-function in developing tissues

nodes.description: Postzygotic activating mutations in FGFR1 occur during early embryonic development, leading to mosaic overactivation of FGFR1 signaling in affected tissues. This drives aberrant proliferation of mesenchymal and neural crest-derived cells, producing lipomas, cutaneous hamartomas, and CNS anomalies in a mosaic distribution. ECCL shares significant phenotypic overlap with mosaic RASopathies due to convergent activation of the RAS-MAPK pathway.
nodes.id: Somatic FGFR1 gain-of-function in developing tissues

evidence.snippet: Encephalocraniocutaneous lipomatosis (ECCL) is a rare neurocutaneous disorder caused by somatic FGFR1 and KRAS variants.
next-generation sequencing of affected tissue identified a pathologic FGFR1 p.K656E variant, thereby establishing a molecular diagnosis.

nodes.description: Activating missense variant in the kinase domain of FGFR1 identified in affected tissue.
